source: OpenGlaze compatibility.py, Digitalfire, Ceramic Arts Network, studio testing
caveats: These rules are probabilistic, not guaranteed. Many glazes break these rules successfully.
Application thickness, firing schedule, and kiln position affect outcomes significantly.
Test small batches before committing to production layering combinations.
Some of the most beautiful glaze combinations come from 'breaking the rules.'

shino_crawl_risk.description: Shino glazes have high crawl risk when layered over non-shino glazes due to different thermal expansion and high flux content
shino_crawl_risk.conditions: Applied as top layer over non-shino base
shino_crawl_risk.mitigation: Apply very thin, reverse layer order (non-shino over shino), or use shino-to-shino layering

zinc_interactions.description: Zinc oxide disrupts several color development mechanisms

chrome_tin_pink.problem: Zinc destroys chrome-tin pink color development, turning pink to brown
chrome_tin_pink.threshold: ZnO > 0.02 in UMF with SnO2 > 0.02 and Cr2O3 > 0.001

copper_shift.problem: Zinc can shift copper green toward blue or cause unpredictable color shifts

iron_copper_suppression.description: Iron in one layer suppresses copper color development in adjacent layer

base_iron_top_copper.problem: Iron in base layer suppresses copper red development in top layer
base_iron_top_copper.threshold: Fe2O3 > 0.05 in base with CuO > 0.01 in top

top_iron_base_copper.problem: Iron in top layer may muddy copper colors in base layer
top_iron_base_copper.threshold: Fe2O3 > 0.05 in top with CuO > 0.01 in base

combined_iron.problem: Very high combined iron makes result too dark/black

thermal_expansion_mismatch.description: Mismatch in thermal expansion between layers causes crazing or shivering

medium.likely_outcome: Possible fine crazing or slight shivering. May be acceptable for decorative ware.

high.likely_outcome: Likely crazing, shivering, or flaking. Test before use.

thermal_expansion_mismatch.cone_notes: Cone 10 reduction has wider thermal tolerance than oxidation firing. These thresholds assume cone 10 reduction.

fluidity_mismatch.description: Large differences in glaze fluidity (SiO2:Al2O3 ratio) between layers cause crawling or running

rules.condition: Top much stiffer than base (ratio difference > 2)
rules.risk: Top glaze may crawl where applied thin

rules.condition: Top much more fluid than base (ratio difference > 2)
rules.risk: Top glaze may run off edges

cobalt_manganese.description: Cobalt + manganese produces purple/brown — unpredictable but often interesting

chrome_contamination.description: Chromium can migrate and discolor surrounding glazes in the kiln
chrome_contamination.note: Affects nearby pieces, not just layered glazes
